title: 3 Establishing Policies for File Exchange and Report Distribution
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for File Exchange and Report Distribution
3.1	Policy on Internal Controls and User Management for the File and Report Exchange
Your financial institution is responsible for File and Report Exchange security practices and internal controls. It is recommended that policies relating to the use of the File and Report Exchange be established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner and to restrict access to the File and Report Exchange. Such policies can include but arenât limited to
identifying and deactivating inactive File and Report Exchange users;
limiting File and Report Exchange access to individuals who require the File and Report Exchange to complete their job requirements;
training individuals not to share user IDs; and
prohibiting the use of multiple user IDs for an individual.
3.2	Policy on Downloading and Saving Files
Files are available on Central 1âs File and Report Exchange for 30 calendar days. The reports from the CRF* and ICR1 files will be purged after seven calendar days since these are advance versions of existing Canadian AFT clearing reports. Establish a policy and internal procedure for downloading and saving files to a separate storage location. Itâs a good practice to download files daily.
END
